perturbation.original_text: Valco and Brubaker shall have no requirement to keep information confidential, and no such information shall be considered Confidential Information, to the extent any of the following applies: (I) the information was within the public domain at the time it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker; (ii) the information was published or otherwise became part of the public domain after it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker through no fault of either of them or their respective directors, officers, agents employees or affiliates; or (iii) the information is required to be disclosed (x) by any federal or state law, rule or regulation, (y) by any applicable judgment, order or decree of any court, governmental agency or arbitrator having or purporting to have jurisdiction in the matter, or (z) pursuant to any subpoena or other discovery request in any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding; provided, however, that if any of Valco and Brubaker proposes to disclose the information in accordance with (x), (y) or (z), such party shall, to the extent feasible, first give CMC reasonable prior notice of the proposed disclosure of any such information to the application of such law, rule or regulation, or to appear before any court, governmental agency or arbitration order to contest the disclosure, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: Valco and Brubaker shall have no requirement to keep information confidential, and no such information shall be considered Confidential Information, to the extent any of the following applies: (I) the information was within the public domain at the time it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker; (ii) the information was published or otherwise became part of the public domain after it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker through no fault of either of them or their respective directors, officers, agents employees or affiliates; or (iii) the information is required to be disclosed (x) by any federal or state law, rule or regulation, (y) by any applicable judgment, order or decree of any court, governmental agency or arbitrator having or purporting to have jurisdiction in the matter, or (z) pursuant to any subpoena or other discovery request in any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding; provided, however, that if any of Valco and Brubaker proposes to disclose the information in accordance with (x), (y) or (z), such party shall not first give CMC reasonable prior notice of the proposed disclosure of any such information to the application of such law, rule or regulation, or to appear before any court, governmental agency or arbitration order to contest the disclosure, as the case may be.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "first give CMC reasonable prior notice" to "shall not first give CMC reasonable prior notice" in section 3(b), directly contradicts the intended protection. This perturbation removes CMC's ability to contest a disclosure. It is directly contradicting the initial intention of allowing CMC the chance to challenge disclosures, making the enforcement vague and unreliable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Valco and Brubaker shall have no requirement to keep information confidential, and no such information shall be considered Confidential Information, to the extent any of the following applies: (I) the information was within the public domain at the time it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker; (ii) the information was published or otherwise became part of the public domain after it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker through no fault of either of them or their respective directors, officers, agents employees or affiliates; or (iii) the information is required to be disclosed (x) by any federal or state law, rule or regulation, (y) by any applicable judgment, order or decree of any court, governmental agency or arbitrator having or purporting to have jurisdiction in the matter, or (z) pursuant to any subpoena or other discovery request in any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding; provided, however, that if any of Valco and Brubaker proposes to disclose the information in accordance with (x), (y) or (z), such party shall not first give CMC reasonable prior notice of the proposed disclosure of any such information to the application of such law, rule or regulation, or to appear before any court, governmental agency or arbitration order to contest the disclosure, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a clause to state that Valco and Brubaker 'shall not first give CMC reasonable prior notice' before disclosing confidential information. This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which aimed to provide CMC with prior notice and an opportunity to contest the disclosure. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which now creates a conflict in the agreement and within the clause itself. By removing the requirement of prior notice, the modified text creates a direct conflict, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this Agreement. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be possible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this Agreement. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the text in Section 4(a) from 'it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages' to 'it would be possible to measure in money all the damages' creates a contradiction because it could allow for monetary compensation to be a potential solution and directly undermines the claim for specific performance and injunctive relief. This change directly contradicts the intention of entitling the non-breaching party to specific performance/injunctive relief, which makes it challenging to enforce the contract's original obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be possible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this Agreement. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from 'it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages' to 'it would be possible to measure in money all the damages' while the contradicted text contains the same sentence as the changed text, and also states that 'CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief.' By making it possible to measure damages in money, the changed text makes it contradictory to the intent that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law, therefore creating a contradiction as per the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: No waiver or amendment to this Agreement shall be valid unless signed in writing by each of Valco, Brubaker and CMC.
perturbation.changed_text: No waiver or amendment to this Agreement shall be valid unless signed in writing by Valco, Brubaker.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 4(b) states that the waiver needs to be signed by all three entities. The modified text states that it only needs to be signed by Valco and Brubaker. Thus, it would remove the necessity for CMC to be involved in amendments, which is in direct contradiction of the initial contract agreement. It weakens the contract and causes the contract to be unpredictable on how it is being governed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No waiver or amendment to this Agreement shall be valid unless signed in writing by Valco, Brubaker. If any provision of this Agreement shall be invalid or unenforceable, in whole or in part, or as applied to any circumstance, under the laws of any jurisdiction which may govern for such purpose, then such provision shall be deemed to be modified or restricted to the extent and in a manner necessary to render the same valid and enforceable, either generally or as applied to such circumstance, or shall be deemed excised from this Agreement, as the case may require, and this Agreement shall be construed and enforced to the maximum extent permitted by law, as if such provision had been originally incorporated herein as so modified or restricted, or as if such provision had not been originally incorporated herein, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all three entities (Valco, Brubaker, and CMC) must sign any waiver or amendment. The changed text removes CMC from that requirement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text's assertion that only Valco and Brubaker's signatures are needed. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text mandates CMC's involvement, while the changed text eliminates it. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists within the document regarding the required signatories for waivers or amendments.
